anchor: ChatGPT Pro and Business frame GPT access in ChatGPT as unlimited or effectively unlimited, subject to guardrails.
note: The latest plan pages no longer clearly support the earlier unlimited-with-guardrails phrasing. Methods: openai-pro-plan:browser-capture, openai-business-limits:browser-capture.

anchor: GPT-5.4 Pro remains the frontier OpenAI model page with a 1,050,000 token context window claim.
note: The latest GPT-5.4 Pro page loads, but a real rendered or browser-backed confirmation is still needed to restate the frontier or 1.05M wording precisely. Methods: openai-gpt-5.4-model:direct-fetch.

anchor: This repo should keep GPT-5.4 as the strategic frontier target while treating Codex-surface defaults as a separately verified runtime fact.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openai-gpt-5.4-model:direct-fetch, openai-codex-upgrades:manual-unverified, openclaw-changelog:manual-unverified, openclaw-pr-36905:manual-unverified.

anchor: OpenClaw GPT-5.4 provider support should be preferred today, while provider-model promotion still benefits from a live authenticated runtime probe.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-model-providers:manual-unverified, openclaw-changelog:manual-unverified, openclaw-pr-36590:manual-unverified, openclaw-pr-36929:manual-unverified.

anchor: Codex supports ChatGPT sign-in and plan-based access.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openai-codex-plan:browser-capture, openai-codex-upgrades:manual-unverified.

anchor: Codex plan usage windows are separate from ChatGPT chat usage and require pacing discipline.
note: The current Codex plan article could not be parsed cleanly enough to restate the present usage-window numbers automatically. Methods: openai-codex-plan:browser-capture.

anchor: OpenClaw officially supports Codex OAuth or ChatGPT sign-in for external workflows.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-model-providers:manual-unverified.

anchor: OpenClaw recommends Node for stable gateways and does not recommend Bun for gateway installs.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-cli:manual-unverified.

anchor: The strongest always-on OpenClaw pattern is one primary loopback-bound gateway with secure remote access and remote nodes where needed.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-multiple-gateways:manual-unverified, openclaw-remote-access:manual-unverified, openclaw-nodes:manual-unverified.

anchor: Cron and heartbeat serve different purposes and should be combined with explicit wake or continuation flows.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-cron-vs-heartbeat:manual-unverified, openclaw-nodes:manual-unverified.

anchor: OpenClaw memory is Markdown-first, and Codex OAuth does not satisfy embeddings for memory search.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-memory:manual-unverified.

anchor: OpenClaw supports QMD and local-first memory search backends.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-memory:manual-unverified.

anchor: OpenClaw skills, plugins, and skill folders are supply-chain risk and must be treated as trusted or untrusted code accordingly.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-security:manual-unverified, openclaw-secrets:manual-unverified.

anchor: OpenClaw current docs expose strict config requirements, additive SecretRefs, and a first-class PDF tool.

anchor: OpenClaw can use a managed browser, attached Chrome relay, and remote nodes; headless mode can trigger more anti-bot friction.

anchor: Automated self-update is possible but not the primary recommended operating pattern.

anchor: Wise personal tokens and OAuth 2.0 support different automation realities, and EU or UK PSD2 rules constrain some personal-token actions.
